notes: SHIP v1.14.267 - FLOOR TILE V2 (per PUNCH-FLOOR-TILE-V2.md; owner screenshot review of .266). Clears P0-P3, 6 edits in rackElevation_render3D, the JOHN-LOCKED §A light rig UNTOUCHED. ROOT CAUSE (P0+P1 are ONE bug, found by isolation-harness repro, not by eye): the floor was a LIT surface. MeshStandard metalness 0.5/roughness 0.35 took a specular lobe from keyL (0xd4e5f7 @0.78, high front-right) -> a bright pool that SWEPT with camera theta = the 'bright state' (there is NO LIGHTS toggle in the file - grepped; neither branch of the punch's P0 conditional applied. Same build, different orbit angle), tinted by rimL 0x00d4aa = the off-palette MINT the owner flagged; and keyL's shadow of the rack CUT that pool left of the base = P1's 'dark angular polygon' - an ABSENCE OF HIGHLIGHT, not a gap in the tile (keyL at (5.45,13.79,4.91) throws it exactly where the owner saw it). FIX (owner's own law, literally): floorMesh -> MeshBasicMaterial = unlit. Floor now takes NO direct light, NO env, NO shadow; it can only show its tile + the ghost clone through its transparency. 'The floor only ever REFLECTS.' Kills the view-angle dependence at source and makes the floor deterministic. receiveShadow dropped (was the P1 source; no-op on Basic). SECOND, INDEPENDENT BUG (mine, from .266, found while reading): _floorTex was the ONLY CanvasTexture in the file left at default LinearEncoding (:18070/:18188/:18197 all set sRGB) -> the authored charcoal was read as already-linear and gamma-LIFTED on output to ~rgb(56) grey. The tile has NEVER rendered at the value .266 specced; that lift was half the 'washed' read. Set encoding=sRGBEncoding -> WYSIWYG. Consequence, reported: the old 10,13,18 / grout #04060a were silently compensating for the lift, so once corrected they went near-invisible against the #04060a void (grout WAS the void colour exactly) - re-authored to base 24,29,37 / grout #090c11 = the same tile landing where 10,13,18 was always meant to. P2 tile pitch: divisor RW*2 -> RW (one tile ~= HALF a rack width, seams denser). NOTE: the .266 math was already ~1 rack width/tile (48U: 81.6/7 = 11.66 per canvas / 2 = 5.83 ~= RW 6.0) - the '2x' read is perspective at elev 0.18, not a maths error; owner's eye is the gate so it goes smaller. 'Halve the repeat' taken as halve the PITCH (= double the count); halving the count would have made tiles BIGGER. P3 presence: clone opacity 0.16->0.22 + floor opacity 0.85->0.80 (both owner-named values; satin, never mirror). VERIFICATION: built an isolation harness (same vendored r128, §A rig verbatim, .265 env, real floor code, stand-in rack) - it REPRODUCES the bright pool, the mint tint and the shadow wedge on the .266 recipe and clears all three on this one; tile reads continuous with grout seams and no visible floor rim. That harness is NOT the app: it uses a stand-in rack, so the live tray/reflection balance is still the owner's eye. BAY_REFLECT/void/mirror/gesture/anisotropy/jitter/fog-dissolve unchanged. reh3d-only -> ?legacy=1 byte-identical; forge3d untouched. Gates: node --check 4/0; CSS balanced; CRLF preserved; three-stamp .266->.267. John iPhone gate: [1] orbit a FULL 360 - the floor must NEVER flare into a bright pool at any angle (this is the P0 regression test; one sweep is the whole check). [2] no dark wedge left of the rack base; floor reads continuous. [3] tile visible as tile - grout seams + per-tile variance read as material, not a checkerboard, and not invisible (if the floor now reads too dark/dead, the lever is the tile base 24,29,37, NOT the lights). [4] lit trays stand faintly in the polish at the base - satin; if still short, next step is clone 0.22->0.26. [5] no mint anywhere on the floor. [6] floor edge still dissolves to void; grout straight, no shimmer. [7] 55+fps for 2min.
